generatedSentence: On the mainland, the underfunded developer, plainly neurotic, adjusted his jaw, a nervous tic betraying him as he glanced at the upgraded schematics for the underpass. A classy novelty sandwich, garnished with oysters, sat next to a bootleg revolver with a sharper barrel. The relentless users, demanding a clearer sentence regarding the project's maternity ward integration, subjected him to a pantomime of questions, threatening to wrack his reputation. He felt the bootjack of their pressure. The developer restarted his computer, a mindless mechanism designed to objectify data, hoping to verify the nanostructure classification. He tentatively picked at a peeled flamingo sticker on his fridge, a nostalgic playtime relic, while his hypothalamus pulsed with anxiety about tomorrow's presentation. He'd never truly kickstarted the project's timber framework, fearing the subtle machinations and kleptomania of rival historians. The butterfly of hope had only a faint twinkle; his big schnozz twitched with stress. The ancient jurisdiction of judges, with their jaguars and runners, would surely deject him, especially if they misjudged his maneuver. He watched veejays on vans project dancing lanterns, distracting him from the cent he owed, the illegally acquired eggs baking in the forest. A vaquero, hooking hickory branches, inadvertently caused a firework to snap, reminding him of his unfaithful fellows. This parameter, a palpable tic of stress, caused him to spazzes. However, his agility would prove crucial to accomplish his goal; he had to acknowledge the concord's relevance. He applied himself, like an acorn rejuvenates a mighty oak, to juggle the remaining tasks. The glittering jewelry and poplins of the kings and knights in the catacomb of skyscrapers would be his offspring to don, a bejewel reward for his efforts. He wouldn't let others objectify his pussy, but rather lead his fellows to a new playtime, a successful lapdance under the city's twinkling lights.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone, gaunt developer, wearing a worn, patched-up lab coat and oversized spectacles, with a prominent, twitching schnozz. TOP-LEFT PANEL: A close-up, low-angle shot of the developer's face, jaw clenched, eyes anxiously glancing at a holographic schematic. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A wide shot showing the developer at a cluttered desk, a flickering screen displaying complex nanostructure classifications, a flamingo sticker peeling from a mini-fridge in the background. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: A medium shot, outside, revealing the developer nervously observing a line of sleek, futuristic vans projecting dancing lanterns against a backdrop of ancient-looking judges and their runners. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: A high-angle, triumphant shot of the developer standing atop a gleaming skyscraper, looking out over a futuristic city skyline adorned with glittering jewelry and poplins, a single firework exploding in the distance.

definitions.definition: Terminating in a point or edge, especially one that can cut easily; not obtuse or rounded.
definitions.example: A face with sharp features

definitions.definition: Intelligent.
definitions.example: My nephew is a sharp lad; he can count to 100 in six languages, and he's only five years old.

definitions.definition: Higher than usual by one semitone (denoted by the symbol ♯ after the name of the note).
Higher in pitch than required.
definitions.example: The orchestra's third violin several times was sharp about an eighth of a tone.

definitions.definition: Having an intense, acrid flavour.
definitions.example: Milly couldn't stand sharp cheeses when she was pregnant, because they made her nauseated.

definitions.definition: Sudden and intense.
definitions.example: A pregnant woman during labor normally experiences a number of sharp contractions.

definitions.definition: Illegal or dishonest.
definitions.example: Michael had a number of sharp ventures that he kept off the books.

definitions.definition: Keenly or unduly attentive to one's own interests; shrewd.
definitions.example: a sharp dealer;  a sharp customer

definitions.definition: Exact, precise, accurate; keen.
definitions.example: You'll need sharp aim to make that shot.

definitions.definition: Offensive, critical, or acrimonious.
definitions.example: When the two rivals met, first there were sharp words, and then a fight broke out.

definitions.definition: Stylish or attractive.
definitions.example: You look so sharp in that tuxedo!

definitions.definition: Observant; alert; acute.
definitions.example: Keep a sharp watch on the prisoners. I don't want them to escape!

definitions.definition: Forming a small angle; especially, forming an angle of less than ninety degrees.
definitions.example: Drive down Main for three quarters of a mile, then make a sharp right turn onto Pine.

definitions.definition: Steep; precipitous; abrupt.
definitions.example: a sharp ascent or descent; a sharp turn or curve

definitions.definition: (of a statement) Said of as extreme a value as possible.
definitions.example: Sure, any planar graph can be five-colored. But that result is not sharp: in fact, any planar graph can be four-colored. That is sharp: the same can't be said for any lower number.

definitions.definition: Tactical; risky.
Piercing; keen; severe; painful.
definitions.example: a sharp pain; the sharp and frosty winter air

definitions.definition: Eager or keen in pursuit; impatient for gratification.
definitions.example: a sharp appetite

definitions.definition: Fierce; ardent; fiery; violent; impetuous.
Composed of hard, angular grains; gritty.
Uttered in a whisper, or with the breath alone; aspirated; unvoiced.
Hungry.
A swindler; a cheat; a professional gambler who makes his living by cheating.
A dish or foodstuff where two or more slices of bread serve as the wrapper or container of some other food.
(by extension) Any combination formed by layering one type of material between two layers of some other material.
A layer cake or sandwich cake.
To place one item between two other, usually flat, items
To put or set something between two others, in time.
Of a meal or serving size that is smaller than a dinner.
A handgun with a revolving chamber enabling several shots to be fired without reloading.
(by extension) Any (personal) firearm with such a mechanism.
Synonym of revolving line of credit
Agent noun of revolve; something that revolves.
Unrelenting or unyielding in severity.
Unremitting, steady and persistent.
In a plain manner; simply; basically.
definitions.example: She decorated the room plainly but neatly.

definitions.definition: Obviously; clearly.
definitions.example: You will see that ours is plainly the better method.

definitions.definition: A Classical comic actor, especially one who works mainly through gesture and mime.
The drama in ancient Greece and Rome featuring such performers; or (later) any of various kinds of performance modelled on such work.

A traditional theatrical entertainment, originally based on the commedia dell'arte, but later aimed mostly at children and involving physical comedy, topical jokes, call and response, and fairy-tale plots.
Gesturing without speaking; dumb-show, mime.
To make (a gesture) without speaking.
definitions.example: I pantomimed steering a car; he understood, and tossed the keys to me.

definitions.definition: To entertain others by silent gestures or actions.
To look briefly (at something).
definitions.example: She glanced at her reflection as she passed the mirror.

definitions.definition: To graze a surface.
To sparkle.
definitions.example: The spring sunlight was glancing on the water of the pond.

definitions.definition: To move quickly, appearing and disappearing rapidly; to be visible only for an instant at a time; to move interruptedly; to twinkle.
To strike and fly off in an oblique direction; to dart aside.
To hit lightly with the head, make a deft header.
To make an incidental or passing reflection; to allude; to hint; often with at.

A type of interaction between parent fish and offspring in which juveniles swim toward and rapidly touch the sides of the parent, in most cases feeding on parental mucus. Relatively few species glance, mainly some Cichlidae.
One who uses or makes use of something, a consumer/client or an express or implied licensee (free user) or a trespasser.
A person who uses drugs, especially illegal drugs.
A person who uses a computer or a computing network, especially a person who has received a user account.
An exploiter, an abuser (a person who "uses" people, that is treats and regards people unfairly, selfishly and/or unethically).

In land law, meaning either 1. or 2. above or use. Usually in singular form to mean use wherever there is assiduous re-use of precedents and aloof textbooks verbatim. Modern law, guarded against ambiguity, widely disfavors the term.
A passage that crosses a road, railroad or similar obstacle in a tunnel underneath it.
To improve, usually applied to technology, generally by complete replacement of one or more components
definitions.example: When you upgrade your standard of living, you become addicted to that standard of living.

definitions.definition: To replace with something better.
definitions.example: I had to upgrade my anti-virus software to protect my computer from the newer threats.

definitions.definition: To improve the equipment or furnishings of or services rendered to
definitions.example: They upgraded him to the latest model.

definitions.definition: To improve in condition or status.
definitions.example: She upgraded to a more successful husband.

definitions.definition: To replace a program with a later version of itself, a version having a higher version number or marketed under a more recent product name.
Having had an upgrade; having been improved or enhanced.
Insufficiently funded.
A V-shaped, or forked, device for pulling off boots.
To steal
The part of a boot that is above the instep.
An illegally produced, transported or sold product; contraband.
An unauthorized recording, e.g., of a live concert.
A remix or mashup that is a combination of two songs but that is not authorized and audited for copyright use; primarily in the electronic music scene.
A play in which the quarterback fakes a handoff, conceals the ball against his hip, and rolls out.
To make, transport and/or sell illegal alcoholic liquor.
To make, transport and/or sell an illegal version or copy of a copyrighted product.
To engage in bootlegging.
Illegally produced, transported or sold; pirated.
A sudden, nonrhythmic motor movement or vocalization.
(by extension) Something that is done or produced habitually or characteristically.
(abbreviation) ticket
To exhibit a tic; to undergo a sudden, semi-voluntary muscle movement.
The state of being a mother; motherhood.
The state of being pregnant; pregnancy.
A ward or department in a hospital in which babies are born.
The continent; the principal land, as distinguished from islands or a peninsula.
The principal island of a group.
A person who has a neurosis
Affected with a neurosis.

definitions.definition: Useful in disorders of, or affecting, the nerves.

A new product; an innovation.
A small mass-produced trinket.
In novelty theory, newness, density of complexification, and dynamic change as opposed to static habituation.
Elegant, highly stylish or fashionable.

definitions.definition: Of a superior type; especially, exhibiting admirable personal qualities.
definitions.example: That was a classy response.

definitions.definition: Any of certain marine bivalve mollusks, especially those of the family Ostreidae (the true oysters), usually found adhering to rocks or other fixed objects in shallow water along the seacoasts, or in brackish water in the mouth of rivers.
The delicate morsel of dark meat contained in a small cavity of the bone on each side of the lower part of the back of a fowl.
A pale beige color tinted with grey or pink, like that of an oyster.
(by analogy) A person who keeps secrets.
A shoplifter.
The decision or judgement of a jury or court; a verdict.
definitions.example: The court returned a sentence of guilt in the first charge, but innocence in the second.

definitions.definition: The judicial order for a punishment to be imposed on a person convicted of a crime.
definitions.example: The judge declared a sentence of death by hanging for the infamous cattle rustler.

definitions.definition: A punishment imposed on a person convicted of a crime.
A saying, especially from a great person; a maxim, an apophthegm.

(grammar) A grammatically complete series of words consisting of a subject and predicate, even if one or the other is implied, and typically beginning with a capital letter and ending with a full stop.
definitions.example: The children were made to construct sentences consisting of nouns and verbs from the list on the chalkboard.

definitions.definition: A formula with no free variables.
Any of the set of strings that can be generated by a given formal grammar.
Sense; meaning; significance.
One's opinion; manner of thinking.
A pronounced opinion or judgment on a given question.
To declare a sentence on a convicted person; to doom; to condemn to punishment.
definitions.example: The judge sentenced the embezzler to ten years in prison, along with a hefty fine.

definitions.definition: To decree or announce as a sentence.
To utter sententiously.
A tube of mirrors containing loose coloured beads etc. that is rotated to produce a succession of symmetrical designs.
A constantly changing set of colours, or other things.
To move in shifting patterns.
To modify.
definitions.example: Morimoto's recipes are adjusted to suit the American palate.

definitions.definition: To improve or rectify.
definitions.example: He adjusted his initial conclusion to reflect the new data.

definitions.definition: To settle an insurance claim.
To change to fit circumstances.
definitions.example: Most immigrants adjust quickly to a new community.   She waited for her eyes to adjust to the darkness.

definitions.definition: Vengeance; revenge; persecution; punishment; consequence; trouble.
(except in dialects) Ruin; destruction.
The remains; a wreck.
To execute vengeance; avenge.
To worry; tease; torment.
One of the bones, usually bearing teeth, which form the framework of the mouth.
The part of the face below the mouth.
definitions.example: His jaw dropped in amazement.

definitions.definition: Anything resembling the jaw of an animal in form or action; especially plural, the mouth or way of entrance.
definitions.example: the jaws of a pass; the jaws of darkness; the jaws of death.

definitions.definition: A notch or opening.
A notched or forked part, adapted for holding an object in place.
definitions.example: the jaw of a railway-car pedestal.

definitions.definition: One of a pair of opposing parts which are movable towards or from each other, for grasping or crushing anything between them.
definitions.example: the jaws of a vise; the jaws of a stone-crushing machine.

definitions.definition: The inner end of a boom or gaff, hollowed in a half circle so as to move freely on a mast.
Impudent or abusive talk.
Axle guard.
The curved part of the cushion marking the entry to the pocket.

Time for play or diversion.

definitions.definition: A duration of time when one is not as serious as they could be, especially in a conflict of sorts.
definitions.example: I thought I could go easy on you and win, but I suppose playtime is over.

definitions.definition: The duration of a recording.
definitions.example: The longest track on the album has a playtime of eight minutes.

definitions.definition: To remove the skin or outer covering of.
definitions.example: I sat by my sister's bed, peeling oranges for her.

definitions.definition: To remove something from the outer or top layer of.
definitions.example: I peeled (the skin from) a banana and ate it hungrily.

definitions.definition: To become detached, come away, especially in flakes or strips; to shed skin in such a way.
definitions.example: I had been out in the sun too long, and my nose was starting to peel.

definitions.definition: To remove one's clothing.
definitions.example: The children peeled by the side of the lake and jumped in.

definitions.definition: To move, separate (off or away).
definitions.example: The scrum-half peeled off and made for the touchlines.

definitions.definition: To play a peel shot.
To send through a hoop (of a ball other than one's own).
To plunder; to pillage, rob.
With the outermost layer or skin removed.
definitions.example: He stirred the campfire stew with a peeled stick, so the bark wouldn't get in it.

definitions.definition: To grasp and pull with the fingers or fingernails.
definitions.example: Don't pick at that scab.

definitions.definition: To harvest a fruit or vegetable for consumption by removing it from the plant to which it is attached; to harvest an entire plant by removing it from the ground.
definitions.example: It's time to pick the tomatoes.

definitions.definition: To pull apart or away, especially with the fingers; to pluck.

definitions.definition: To take up; especially, to gather from here and there; to collect; to bring together.
definitions.example: to pick rags

definitions.definition: To remove something from somewhere with a pointed instrument, with the fingers, or with the teeth.
definitions.example: to pick the teeth; to pick a bone; to pick a goose; to pick a pocket

definitions.definition: To decide upon, from a set of options; to select.
definitions.example: I'll pick the one with the nicest name.

definitions.definition: To seek (a fight or quarrel) where the opportunity arises.

definitions.definition: To pluck the individual strings of a musical instrument or to play such an instrument.

definitions.definition: To steal; to pilfer.

Chosen; selected.

To start a motorcycle.

A person who decides the fate of someone or something that has been called into question.

definitions.definition: A person who evaluates something or forms an opinion.

definitions.definition: To sit in judgment on; to pass sentence on.

definitions.definition: To sit in judgment, to act as judge.
definitions.example: Justices in this country judge without appeal.

definitions.definition: To form an opinion on.

definitions.definition: To arbitrate; to pass opinion on something, especially to settle a dispute etc.

definitions.definition: To have as an opinion; to consider, suppose.
definitions.example: I judge it safe to leave the house once again.

definitions.definition: To form an opinion; to infer.

definitions.definition: To criticize or label another person or thing.
Act or instance of running, of moving rapidly using the feet.

definitions.definition: Act or instance of hurrying (to or from a place) (not necessarily by foot); dash or errand, trip.

definitions.definition: A pleasure trip.

definitions.definition: Flight, instance or period of fleeing.
Migration (of fish).
A group of fish that migrate, or ascend a river for the purpose of spawning.
A path taken by literal movement or figuratively
An enclosure for an animal; a track or path along which something can travel.

definitions.definition: Rural landholding for farming, usually for running sheep, and operated by a runholder.
State of being current; currency; popularity.
Continuous or sequential
A flow of liquid; a leak.

definitions.definition: (West Virginia) A small creek or part thereof. (Compare Southern US branch and New York and New England brook.)

definitions.definition: A quick pace, faster than a walk.

definitions.definition: A sudden series of demands on a bank or other financial institution, especially characterised by great withdrawals.

definitions.definition: Any sudden large demand for something.

definitions.definition: Various horizontal dimensions or surfaces
A standard or unexceptional group or category.

definitions.definition: In sports
A line of knit stitches that have unravelled, particularly in a nylon stocking.

definitions.definition: The stern of the underwater body of a ship from where it begins to curve upward and inward.

The horizontal distance to which a drift may be carried, either by licence of the proprietor of a mine or by the nature of the formation; also, the direction which a vein of ore or other substance takes.
A pair or set of millstones.

definitions.definition: A deserter.
A type of soft-soled shoe originally intended for runners.
Part of a shoe that is stitched to the bottom of the upper so it can be glued to the sole.
A part of an apparatus that moves quickly.

definitions.definition: A mechanical part intended to guide or aid something else to move (using wheels or sliding).
An automobile; a working or driveable automobile.

definitions.definition: A strip of fabric used to decorate or protect a table or dressing table.

definitions.definition: A long, narrow carpet for a high traffic area such as a hall or stairs.

definitions.definition: A part of a cigarette that is burning unevenly.
A long stolon sent out by a plant (such as strawberry), in order to root new plantlets, or a plant that propagates by using such runners.
A short sling with a carabiner on either end, used to link the climbing rope to a bolt or other protection such as a nut or friend.
A competitor in a poker tournament.

A leaping food fish (Elagatis pinnulatis) of Florida and the West Indies; the skipjack, shoemaker, or yellowtail.

A rope to increase the power of a tackle.
A speedrunner.
An idea or plan that has potential to be adopted or put into operation.

definitions.definition: Running shoes, sneakers, trainers
In a manner contrary to law.

definitions.definition: A cat.

The female genitalia; the vulva and/or vagina.

Make sad or dispirited.
To cast down.

A case of translucent or transparent material made to protect a flame, or light, used to illuminate its surroundings.
Especially, a metal casing with lens used to illuminate a stage (e.g. spotlight, floodlight).
An open structure of light material set upon a roof, to give light and air to the interior.

A perforated barrel to form a core upon.

A covered vehicle used for carrying goods or people, usually roughly cuboid in shape, longer and higher than a car but smaller than a truck/lorry.

definitions.definition: An enclosed railway vehicle for transport of goods.
A light wagon, either covered or open, used by tradesmen and others for the transportation of goods.
A large towable vehicle equipped for the repair of structures that cannot easily be moved.
To transport in a van or similar vehicle (especially of horses).
(used in passive voice) Of law enforcement: to arrest (not necessarily in a van; derived from party van).

A fan or other contrivance, such as a sieve, for winnowing grain.
A wing with which the air is beaten.
The leading units at the front of an army or fleet.
(by extension) The person(s) at the forefront of any group or movement.
Someone who presents a television programme of videos; a video jockey.
Vagina, vulva.
A cowboy; a herdsman.

A toll-free highway.
A dense uncultivated tract of trees and undergrowth, larger than woods.
Any dense collection or amount.

A graph with no cycles; i.e., a graph made up of trees.
A group of domains that are managed as a unit.
The colour forest green.
To cover an area with trees.

To move or go with a sudden turn.
The act of catching or fastening something on a hook.

The wood of these trees.
Of or pertaining to the hickory tree or its wood.

The point in the interior of a sphere that is equidistant from all points on the circumference.
The middle portion of something; the part well away from the edges.
The point on a line that is midway between the ends.

The point in the interior of any figure of any number of dimensions that has as its coordinates the arithmetic mean of the coordinates of all points on the perimeter of the figure (or of all points in the interior for a center of volume).
The subgroup (respectively, subring), denoted Z(G), of those elements of a given group (respectively, ring) G that commute with every element of G.

(Sanskrit and other Indian philology) satakam, set of one hundred verses connected by the same metre or topic

A period of 100 consecutive years; often specifically a numbered period with conventional start and end dates, e.g., the twentieth century, which stretches from (strictly) 1901 through 2000, or (informally) 1900 through 1999. The first century AD was from 1 to 100.

A unit in ancient Roman army, originally of 100 army soldiers as part of a cohort, later of more varied sizes (but typically containing 60 to 70 or 80) soldiers or other men (guards, police, firemen), commanded by a centurion.

A hundred things of the same kind; a hundred.
A hundred runs scored either by a single player in one innings, or by two players in a partnership.

definitions.example: Roughly, a tuple of arguments could be thought of as a vector, whereas a tuple of parameters could be thought of as a covector (i.e., linear functional). When a function is called, a parameter tuple becomes "bound" to an argument tuple, allowing the function instance itself to be computed to yield a return value. This would be roughly analogous to applying a covector to a vector (by taking their dot product (or, rather, matrix-product of row vector and column vector)) to obtain a scalar.

definitions.definition: An actual value given to such a formal parameter (argument or actual parameter).
A characteristic or feature that distinguishes something from others.

(modern) A generic name for various mushrooms belonging to the fungal order gilled mushrooms; scientific name Tricholoma.
To admit the knowledge of; to recognize as a fact or truth; to declare one's belief in
definitions.example: to acknowledge the being of a god

definitions.definition: To own or recognize in a particular quality, character or relationship; to admit the claims or authority of; to give recognition to.
To be grateful of (e.g. a benefit or a favour)
definitions.example: to acknowledge a favor

definitions.definition: To report (the receipt of a message to its sender).
definitions.example: This is to acknowledge your kind invitation to participate in the upcoming debate.

definitions.definition: To own as genuine or valid; to assent to (a legal instrument) to give it validity; to avow or admit in legal form.
Collectively, personal ornamentation such as rings, necklaces, brooches and bracelets, made of precious metals and sometimes set with gemstones.
definitions.example: She had more jewellery ornamented about her than any three ladies needed.
